customer_anxiety_profile: Banks is an older homeowner (26 years in the home) with hearing difficulty and limited tech comfort. He is cooperative and warm but moves slowly and needs things repeated. He is motivated by both bill savings and environmental values, and came in specifically expecting to learn about government incentive programs. He is not anxious or skeptical — he is genuinely interested but will need patience and clear explanations.
unresolved_concerns: Banks asked about 'government help' and incentive programs at the start of the call (01:53) — this was never addressed beyond the bill-swap framing. The closer should expect this question and be prepared to walk through incentive structure.

Banks's credit self-assessment was contradictory ('I think it's less than 670' → 'I've never done a credit score' → 'pretty good credit score'). Credit qualification is unconfirmed and should be handled early in the closer conversation.
Banks said 'at my house here' when confirming the appointment (22:21), suggesting he may expect an in-person visit. The closer should clarify at the start that this is a phone/video appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Banks disclosed hearing difficulty and age ('I'm old, not a little old, and a little hard on hearing') — the closer should speak clearly, avoid rushing, and confirm understanding at each step.
Banks has doctor's appointments on his calendar and checked carefully to confirm the 20th was clear — he is managing a busy medical schedule and the appointment time matters to him.
exact_promises_rep_made: Bucky told Banks he would be assigned to 'the A team' / 'the best' (21:55–21:58).
Bucky emailed Banks at bfshaw2@gmail.com during the call.
closer_should_know: Anne is on the title and was never included in the appointment setup — she is the single biggest structural risk to this sit. The closer should open by asking if Anne is available to join, and if not, should assess whether to proceed or reschedule. Do not assume Banks is the sole decision-maker.
